report_name: Germany's Cyber Commander Warns of Increased Attacks on Defense Supply Chain; Black Basta Implicated in Rheinmetall Ransomware Incident

threat_actors.name: Black Basta
threat_actors.description: A financially motivated, Russian-speaking ransomware group with suspected links to the Russian intelligence ecosystem. The group conducted a ransomware attack against German defense contractor Rheinmetall, timed after announcements of German support for Ukraine.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data theft, with attacks potentially aligned with Russian geopolitical objectives to disrupt and intimidate nations supporting Ukraine.

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: Attacks are being launched against the entire German defense supply chain, targeting industrial companies that design, manufacture, and supply military hardware to disrupt Germany's rearmament efforts.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Black Basta used its ransomware to encrypt data within Rheinmetall's civilian automotive unit, causing significant financial damage and operational disruption.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Black Basta employs double extortion tactics, which involves stealing data for leverage before encrypting systems. This implies data was exfiltrated from Rheinmetall's network.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Implied as a common initial access vector for ransomware groups like Black Basta, especially given the German cyber chief's comment that "human error remains the biggest vulnerability."

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Mentioned as a common initial access method for ransomware groups, involving the exploitation of vulnerabilities in services like VPNs or RDP.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Black Basta and similar ransomware groups are known to use commands like 'wmic.exe shadowcopy delete' to remove volume shadow copies, preventing easy recovery from backups.

malware.name: Black Basta
malware.description: A Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) payload used by the eponymous group. It was deployed in the attack against Rheinmetall to encrypt files as part of a double extortion scheme.

tools.name: Cobalt Strike
tools.description: Mentioned as a commonly used post-exploitation tool for lateral movement and credential theft by ransomware groups like Black Basta.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: Mentioned as a commonly used post-exploitation tool for credential theft by ransomware groups like Black Basta.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: A legitimate administration tool noted as being used by ransomware groups for lateral movement and to deploy ransomware payloads across an enterprise.

tools.name: wmic
tools.description: The Windows Management Instrumentation command-line utility, used by Black Basta to delete volume shadow copies with the 'shadowcopy delete' command to hinder system recovery.

identities.name: Rheinmetall
identities.description: A major German defense contractor whose civilian automotive division was targeted by a Black Basta ransomware attack, resulting in over $10 million in damages. The attack occurred after the company announced plans for a tank factory in Ukraine.

identities.name: German Defense Industry
identities.description: The collective industrial base and supply chain for Germany's military, which is experiencing a surge in cyberattacks amid the country's rearmament and support for Ukraine.

indicators.name: Black Basta Ransomware TTP - WMIC Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Black Basta Ransomware TTP - WMIC Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 9b1f8e4a-1b1a-4b1e-8f0a-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of wmic.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common tactic used by ransomware groups like Black Basta to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/germany-cyber-security-russia-china-1.7200000
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-09
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'shadowcopy'
      - 'delete'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a command-line pattern associated with Black Basta and other ransomware groups using the Windows Management Instrumentation utility to delete volume shadow copies, preventing system recovery.

indicators.name: Black Basta TTP - PsExec for Lateral Movement
indicators.pattern:
title: Black Basta TTP - PsExec for Lateral Movement
id: 8c2d9f5b-2c2b-4c2f-9f1b-2e3f4a5b6c7d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PsExec, a tool commonly used by ransomware groups like Black Basta for lateral movement and remote payload deployment.
references:
  - https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/germany-cyber-security-russia-china-1.7200000
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-09
tags:
  - attack.t1570
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    OriginalFileName: 'psexec.exe'
  filter_legit:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\explorer.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PsExec, a legitimate tool often abused by threat actors like Black Basta for lateral movement within a compromised network.
